a:T4e21,<div class="question">
<p>The graph shows the proportion of a bacterial population of <em>Neisseria gonorrhoeae</em>, displaying resistance to the antibiotic tetracycline.</p>
<p style="text-align:center;"><img src="data:image/png;base64,"/></p>
<p style="text-align:center;">[Source: © All rights reserved. <em>Canadian Antimicrobial Resistance Surveillance System Report, 2016</em>. Public Health Agency of Canada. Adapted and reproduced with permission from the Minister of Health, 2022.]</p>
<p style="text-align:left;"> </p>
<p style="text-align:left;">What can be deduced from this graph?</p>
<p style="text-align:left;">A. Bacteria with beneficial adaptations survive and pass on their genes.</p>
<p style="text-align:left;">B. Immunity to tetracycline is triggered by over-use of the antibiotic.</p>
<p style="text-align:left;">C. Genetic variation in this bacterial population is increasing.</p>
<p style="text-align:left;">D. Use of tetracycline inhibits the growth of antibiotic-resistant <em>N. gonorrhoeae</em>.</p>
</div>b:T40d,<div class="question">
<p>This question attracted the most criticism from teachers on G2 forms. The graph showed fluctuating levels of antibiotic resistance, with an overall trend of increasing resistance. Candidates were expected to understand that antibiotic resistance is a heritable trait, so a change in antibiotic resistance levels is evolution. The explanation of increasing resistance levels is therefore based in natural selection: bacteria with genes for resistance have an advantage so survive, reproduce and pass on their genes. After careful scrutiny of the question, it was decided that there was a second acceptable answer. Although the graph does not provide information on antibiotic use, it was not unreasonable to deduce that using the antibiotic causes evolution by acting as a selective agent. Both answers A and D were therefore marked as correct in order to award the benefit of the doubt. The question has been altered for the published version for the examinations CD, with A being the correct option.</p>
</div>2:["$","article",null,{"className":"q-page","children":[["$","p",null,{"style":{"margin":0},"children":["$","$L6",null,{"href":"/subject/biology/topic/5/","children":["← back to ","Biology"," topic ","5"]}]}],["$","h1",null,{"children":"22M.1.SL.TZ1.21"}],["$","div",null,{"className":"q-meta","children":[["$","span",null,{"className":"tag","children":"pestle"}],["$","span",null,{"children":"Biology"}],null,["$","span",null,{"children":"SL"}],["$","span",null,{"children":["Paper ","1"]}],["$","span",null,{"children":"22M"}],["$","span",null,{"children":["· ","5-2-natural-selection"]}],["$","a",null,{"href":"https://pestle.pages.dev/app/","target":"_blank","rel":"noopener","children":"source ↗"}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"q-content","dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"$a"}}],["$","details",null,{"className":"solution","children":[["$","summary",null,{"children":"Markscheme / solution"}],["$","div",null,{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<div class=\"question\">\n<p>A</p>\n</div>"}}]]}],["$","details",null,{"className":"solution","children":[["$","summary",null,{"children":"Examiners’ report"}],["$","div",null,{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"$b"}}]]}]]}]
